verdict.label: Worth checking
verdict.headline: Almagreira Lunch — 13:00
verdict.reason: Almagreira is worth a quick check for lunch — 13:00, but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  The wind should help keep it tidy.  The tide is part of the picture, but not the deciding factor.

conversationalSummary.text: Almagreira at Lunch — 13:00 looks like the best option, but it is still a check-first call. There is enough potential for a useful session, but the weak source agreement could spoil it. I'd check Surfline or MEO Beachcam before leaving.

best.explanation: Almagreira looks like a clean small north coast window for Rare window, Low confidence: 0.3-0.6 m, 44 kJ, 8 s period, lully 18/100, and good wind at 20 km/h.  An exposed north-coast beach break with more raw swell than Baleal; this is best for small, clean north-coast days when protected spots look underpowered, so checking exposed beaches makes sense.  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully; 6-9s at high tide is usually too fat for exposed beaches like Belgas / Almagreira / Cerro / Meio da Baia.

warnings: 6-9s at high tide is usually too fat for exposed beaches like Belgas / Almagreira / Cerro / Meio da Baia

ranked.explanation: Rey Cortiço looks like a small north coast window for Rare window, Low confidence: 0.3-0.6 m, 59 kJ, 9 s period, lully 31/100, and wind is not fully clear.  An open beach break where the sandbanks decide everything; this is best for quiet, punchy peaks when Lagide is too small, so checking exposed beaches makes sense.  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully; 6-9s at high tide is usually too fat for Belgas.
ranked.shortExplanation: Rey Cortiço is worth a quick check for lunch — 13:00, but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  The wind should be manageable, but not a big advantage.  The tide is fine, but Belgas can still work lower if the rest is better.
warnings: 6-9s at high tide is usually too fat for Belgas

ranked.explanation: Lagide looks like a small window for Fun progression, Low confidence: 0.3-0.6 m, 37 kJ, 9 s period, lully 14/100, and wind is not fully clear.  A dependable left-hand reef that turns modest swell into real rides; this is best for clean, playful walls when the bigger beaches are too wild or too small.  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully; spot surf looks too small; 6-9s at mid tide is soft and sectiony on the reef shelves.
ranked.shortExplanation: Lagide is worth a quick check for afternoon — 16:00, but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  The wind should be manageable, but not a big advantage.  The tide helps a little, but it is not a perfect match.
warnings: 6-9s at mid tide is soft and sectiony on the reef shelves

ranked.explanation: Supertubos looks like a small window for Worth checking, Low confidence: 0.3-0.6 m, 52 kJ, 9 s period, lully 16/100, and wind is the main risk.  A fast, hollow sand-bottom wave that gets serious quickly; this is best for short, punchy bodyboard barrels when it is small enough for comfort; 6-9s at high tide is too flat and weak for Supertubos / Molhe Leste.  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully; spot surf looks too small; wind angle is poor for this spot; 6-9s at high tide is too flat and weak for Supertubos / Molhe Leste.
